description: Capability set for the Indigo-only desktop alternate UX window. The Meetings page self-fetches calendar data via the app's meetings_* commands directly (meetings_list_upcoming, meetings_list_scheduled_bots, meetings_list_accounts, meetings_list_calendars_for_account, meetings_list_memberships) — same as the standalone meetings-window. The Projects surface self-fetches local-PRD data via get_local_projects and get_local_project_prd (US-003, local HQ-tree reader with vault fallback), plus get_local_project_readme (US-009, reads a project's sibling README.md by its prd path with the same path-traversal guard) and get_local_company_goals (reads a company's objectives + initiatives from companies/<slug>/board.json with the same slug/path guard; missing board → empty goals), and persists status changes via set_local_project_status (US-010, atomic + round-trip-validated write to companies/*/board.json) and set_local_story_passes (US-010, atomic toggle of a story's passes in the project's prd.json) — both reuse the same is_within HQ-folder path guard and the Indigo gate. The Library surface self-fetches local skills + workers via get_library_root and get_library_company (read-only scans of core/workers/registry.yaml plus .claude/skills, personal/skills, and companies/<slug>/skills SKILL.md frontmatter), with detail reads get_library_worker_detail (reads a worker's worker.yaml by its registry path) and get_library_skill_detail (reads a SKILL.md's frontmatter + markdown body) — both reuse the same is_within HQ-folder path guard and the Indigo gate. These are app-registered Tauri commands authorized by core:default (custom commands are not gated by per-command permission identifiers in this ACL, only plugins are), so no allow-* tokens exist or are added for them.
